words.definition: a temporary stop or rest, especially in speech or action
words.example: He took a pause before answering the question.
words.exampleText: He took a pause before answering the question.

words.definition: uncomfortable or worried in a situation
words.example: Antonio was very ill at ease during the meeting.
words.exampleText: Antonio was very ill at ease during the meeting.

words.definition: worried or anxious about something
words.example: Paolo looked very troubled after the news.
words.exampleText: Paolo looked very troubled after the news.

words.definition: not formal or planned; relaxed
words.example: He gave a casual wave as he passed by.
words.exampleText: He gave a casual wave as he passed by.

words.definition: smiling brightly
words.example: Old Niccolo's face was beaming with joy.
words.exampleText: Old Niccolo's face was beaming with joy.

words.definition: to move something quickly and suddenly
words.example: Benvenuto flicked his tail sideways.
words.exampleText: Benvenuto flicked his tail sideways.

words.definition: in a smooth and flowing manner
words.example: He moved fluidly across the room.
words.exampleText: He moved fluidly across the room.

words.definition: busy, energetic, and noisy activity
words.example: The bustle in the streets was lively and colourful.
words.exampleText: The bustle in the streets was lively and colourful.

words.definition: moving up and down or back and forth
words.example: Washing flapping overhead made a soft sound.
words.exampleText: Washing flapping overhead made a soft sound.

words.definition: shining brightly, especially with reflected light
words.example: A long gleaming car appeared slowly on the street.
words.exampleText: A long gleaming car appeared slowly on the street.

words.definition: to make a loud sound as a warning, especially a car horn
words.example: The car was hooting loudly to clear the way.
words.exampleText: The car was hooting loudly to clear the way.

words.definition: having or showing an excessive pride in oneself or one's achievements
words.example: He was smug at being able to understand the language.
words.exampleText: He was smug at being able to understand the language.

words.definition: a large number of people gathered together
words.example: The crowd pushed him off the sun once.
words.exampleText: The crowd pushed him off the sun once.

words.definition: feeling or showing anger because of something unfair or wrong
words.example: Tonino was indignant when someone pushed him aside.
words.exampleText: Tonino was indignant when someone pushed him aside.

words.definition: to push someone or something roughly
words.example: "Don't shove!" he shouted.
words.exampleText: "Don't shove!" he shouted.

words.definition: to express an objection to something
words.example: The man protested when others told him to be quiet.
words.exampleText: The man protested when others told him to be quiet.

words.definition: easily excited or made nervous
words.example: He had an odd excitable manner.
words.exampleText: He had an odd excitable manner.

words.definition: shining with a bright reflected light
words.example: He wore glittering medals on his suit.
words.exampleText: He wore glittering medals on his suit.

words.definition: a piece of jewellery that is fastened to clothing
words.example: He held a brooch which winked like a teardrop.
words.exampleText: He held a brooch which winked like a teardrop.

words.definition: a decoration shaped like a rose, often a ribbon or badge
words.example: Golden rosettes adorned his knees.
words.exampleText: Golden rosettes adorned his knees.

words.definition: to make a loud prolonged sound (cry, laughter, etc.)
words.example: Everyone was howling with laughter.
words.exampleText: Everyone was howling with laughter.

words.definition: to deceive someone into doing something or believing something
words.example: Mr. Punch tricked the Hangman into being hanged instead of him.
words.exampleText: Mr. Punch tricked the Hangman into being hanged instead of him.

words.definition: to leave a place, especially to go somewhere quiet or private
words.example: He retired in triumph into the little painted villa.
words.exampleText: He retired in triumph into the little painted villa.

words.definition: in a sad or depressing way
words.example: He sat gloomily on the parapet, gazing across the river.
words.exampleText: He sat gloomily on the parapet, gazing across the river.
